name: m6

env_axis.name: live-round
env_axis.shift_mechanism: forward live-evaluation across 12 monthly rounds spanning 2022 Fed-tightening + inflation regime change

facts: 100 publicly-traded assets (50 stocks + 50 ETFs) × 12 monthly live submission rounds (Q1 2022 – Q1 2023).
hypotheses: The 2022 Fed-tightening / inflation regime did not expose method-quality differentials: returns were so noise-dominated that ranking is effectively random.
M6's forward-live design is a noisy in-distribution test for short-horizon near-martingale returns rather than a genuine OOD test.
intuitions: The M6 results paper (Makridakis et al. 2024) likely documents the winning methods and what they did differently.
Work on M5-vs-M6 transferability and finance-foundation-models probably exists post-2023.
unknowns: There is no established label for 'evaluation under near-irreducible-noise' — methods compete in a regime where signal is a small fraction of variance, and whether any OOD measurement can resolve them within 12 rounds cannot be determined.
epistemic_state.boundary: N=100 assets, 12 rounds — useful only as a forward-live design pattern, not for structural OOD study; a methodological anchor more than a dataset.

representational_commitments: Quintile prediction (5-class) rather than continuous return prediction
